status: TIER 1 PRIMARY-SOURCE BATCH A COMPLETE WITHIN DEFINED SCOPE / SUPPLIER PRODUCT PASSPORT OPEN / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Linear alpha-MSH analogue and authorised implant active substance
mechanism_summary: Melanocortin-1 receptor agonism stimulating eumelanin production. Authorised afamelanotide use is product-, implant- and indication-specific.
evidence_maturity: Authorised medicine and clinical evidence exist for the Scenesse implant in erythropoietic protoporphyria. Cosmetic-tanning use and unverified powder products are separate evidence and regulatory objects.
regulatory_headline: AUTHORISED SCENESSE IMPLANT CONTEXTS EXIST FOR EPP — CATALOGUE POWDER IS NOT AN AUTHORISED-PRODUCT EQUIVALENT
certification_blockers: Supplier powder sequence, salt, potency, purity and sterility
No equivalence to the 16 mg bioresorbable Scenesse implant
source_closure_scope: pivotal or major randomised human evidence
current major safety and contraindication sources
authoritative_product_boundary: SCENESSE is an exact 16 mg afamelanotide subcutaneous implant for adults with erythropoietic protoporphyria, administered by trained healthcare professionals. The European authorisation is under exceptional circumstances and remains subject to post-authorisation monitoring. MT1 powder is not the implant and is not an authorised tanning or cosmetic product.
human_evidence_boundary: Two multicentre randomised double-blind placebo-controlled trials in Europe and the United States evaluated 16 mg afamelanotide implants every 60 days in adults with erythropoietic protoporphyria and supported pain-free light-exposure evidence.
safety_source_boundary: Current controls include trained implantation, hypersensitivity/anaphylaxis, insertion-site monitoring, skin pigmentation changes and periodic full-body skin examination. The authorised evidence does not support unsupervised powder use or cosmetic tanning.
